========================================================================
ARC Test Vectors — draft-ietf-privacypass-arc-crypto-01 (March 2026)
========================================================================

Sources
-------
  Draft (txt):   https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ietf-privacypass-arc-crypto-01.txt
                 Section 10.2, pages 40-43 (lines 2212-2380 of the plaintext).
  WG JSON:       https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-arc/main/poc/vectors/allVectors.json
  WG TXT:        https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-arc/main/poc/vectors/allVectors.txt
  WG generator:  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ietf-wg-privacypass/draft-arc/main/poc/test_arc.sage
  Apple mirror:  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apple/swift-crypto/main/Tests/CryptoExtrasTests/ARC/ARCTestVectors.swift
                 (inlined JSON as a Swift string literal; DIFFERENT VALUES — see note at bottom)

The draft txt, WG JSON, and WG TXT all agree byte-for-byte. Apple's
swift-crypto file uses a different, older seed / PRNG state and therefore
produces completely different scalars and points — do NOT mix them.

Global PRNG seed (from test_arc.sage line 30):
    seed = b"test vector seed" + b"\x00" * 16   (32 bytes, for TestDRNG)

Global presentationLimit (from test_arc.sage line 52): 2
    => k = ceil(log2(2)) = 1, so there is exactly one bit D_0 in the
       range-proof decomposition.

========================================================================
Test Vector: ARCV1-P256
========================================================================

Inputs
------
request_context       (hex, 20 bytes "test request context"):
    74657374207265717565737420636f6e74657874
presentation_context  (hex, 25 bytes "test presentation context"):
    746573742070726573656e746174696f6e20636f6e74657874
presentationLimit:
    2

Server setup
------------
x0  (scalar, 32 B): 1008f2c706ae2157c75e41b2d75695c7bf480d0632a1ef447036cafe4cabb021
x1  (scalar, 32 B): 526e009578f6f25fdec992343f09f5e6c58489c31fcf8a934bbaf85797121bdd
x2  (scalar, 32 B): 549075ccd3d1c36b3546725c43e71943414409a23b980b2c47a3fc2b9c37679b
xb  (scalar, 32 B): 7276533ce3c89f04a007c2e8aa7d2e3b36829d0eaab5631347d8336c2da09a8e
    (xb = x0Blinding)

X0  (point, 33 B):  03bad54cc48293ef3472ac1ada55c9c9fdb3eb99ee47369bbe1d3ce46b300cd7b3
X1  (point, 33 B):  02a0323862a05707d76862bfa8477eed468441ceae14c8fb1659e0b3020b8a24e1
X2  (point, 33 B):  031d16ef08ede5a347e94a8eca071bec7bedb9d8ba943d24bde912a4e1578e529b

CreateCredentialRequest
-----------------------
m1      (scalar): 141c4ca5e614af8e5e323eb47a7e7673ebb67caf49dfa8e109f45f231227f7a0
m2      (scalar): 911fb315257d9ae29d47ecb48c6fa27074dee6860a0489f8db6ac9a486be6a3e
r1      (scalar): 5c183d2dea942eb2780afb90cfd94983ae6575d60e350021c8c93008ac503973
r2      (scalar): 044d4a5b5daf00dd1fb4444ca2f8c3facc95d537d5ad0e0a2815c912e98a431d
m1_enc  (point):  033fe5d950712f711e5d292d68f804fad4c35fb7f3f1866516448647d4aab12590
m2_enc  (point):  026502a833ed1d972ee27175e750b1719adee12726c653125887c0d32b1f3747ab
request proof (hex, 5*Ns = 160 bytes; layout: challenge || responses[0..3]):
    2a088673e302502a3dc80d6100a1bb709083ac7b31da34f9a7c52e7cfeaa2ea3   # challenge
    0b7341133086e64b79dfc6cdac9f348ddbed0b087746f0167ea238d3ddf17e61   # response_0
    3880b73e85f499c7eddc6555355ea71487b49862400091b5b32cb219d7104f57   # response_1
    1306bc6f2487bab299bb2e9a1078dee94d83b6536ed570f8114ee9c97b8b602b   # response_2
    facbeb3764f6a22915a19c24895a6bf7048c663337f7690f0182a1f866586d9e   # response_3

CreateCredentialResponse
------------------------
b             (scalar): 9ac9d836ef405f4c6c1de4de18d210c929a8dc786c95e3eac3a828cc19e1636e
U             (point):  021cf52318c97c33472cc8fb42a5b5a774f83c3b36e6c782209d53e5945d99a493
enc_U_prime   (point):  02ae23020d5427c7f785a72d77c24997f955e66ab7c378c334b7c259dabdf572d7
X0_aux        (point):  031523abe64e436e65e592abdae322dc556fcbea707757e18d4160ba57d574cd87
X1_aux        (point):  023cc3b53807f6e0082b675794ae9f6b370483ca5a3e6d688c3b81f2fdb6d4ec00
X2_aux        (point):  0329dc7c93f8a231a1f16ec69f0fba446e022ce69945b20f37386a7fda3e573b79
H_aux         (point):  0389746891b6dbf062511619eae7d72ae87630bea1e277a925708fdfef8363a1d4

Note: the draft does NOT publish t1/t2. They are the intermediate scalar
products b*x1 and b*x2 used inside server.respond(); not part of the
wire format, so no test vector for them.

response proof (hex, 8*Ns = 256 bytes; layout: challenge || responses[0..6]):
    ec342aee0d481435379ea6bbe919edd5d2eb9c12198a083e0e899da1f14dbc46   # challenge
    a8048f5a12c5cae21e5f5949fe08d1c15c266c63544615400def4ce9a6cf8aee   # response_0
    32052ced26e7a9d854f2c45ea23ffea0f6bf977f6155d412991abc0e2d1ad835   # response_1
    04129c1ac8319b2a45940c52c4b41bde80969313641b9cb727445e20b44d0ea8   # response_2
    84e9b180cd152442883038b97d72772201f281d76a18d22e374bd989accd7654   # response_3
    8067399162428c4d25daf1b7f68f3580a38cc4564a88f28494649064500f06c5   # response_4
    b946dde032a389f8fe337605627ce91a92c20db911100a2c7c42ae15fde5a5cb   # response_5
    d9d078b819a80423593192c40d70ce77f1a6d377770fe5c05781782bd1eaa43f   # response_6

FinalizeCredential
------------------
U       (point):  021cf52318c97c33472cc8fb42a5b5a774f83c3b36e6c782209d53e5945d99a493
U_prime (point):  02646199272c28911165b4d1c5f4ffbd8a83f686948fd4c7250e28c81dbfecd354
X1      (point):  02a0323862a05707d76862bfa8477eed468441ceae14c8fb1659e0b3020b8a24e1
m1      (scalar): 141c4ca5e614af8e5e323eb47a7e7673ebb67caf49dfa8e109f45f231227f7a0
    (the Credential is (m1, U, U_prime, X1); m2 is carried separately by the client)

Presentation 1 (nonce = 0)
--------------------------
a               (scalar): a3c469d2d55062b463b17f45acd2fb17c038b18df4c8d6c9c745866ba961de9a
r               (scalar): 54925f70e9ec2128114c6ae8bfc6e1a2914a8fdd383e5ff03d8c2992edd081a9
z               (scalar): 9ed3c3ddb1b5ff64e55b1d0a4f58eee67351fea7de16baf644e80f4d0949cf5f
nonce_blinding  (scalar): ed8b643e3c8ba74cc417b5bbfc4f42bee6dcd2d6c5ea48fb2273afec7b6505a4
nonce           (int):    0x0
U               (point):  0216af8901c1ad38a703bf9003fabea440b411b4f072fd23b5254cb17d1b5bf33d
U_prime_commit  (point):  03140f8e6f6c5eab3d03a7fba5d542362a9bc00a89d80caa5051b4e4446b0b01f3
m1_commit       (point):  0214d0297c21120d621cc6fed75852569de3cbf0bd9f5a8a812cf6b024bf51e627
tag             (point):  0281428e61688f4e7989dbe8dab170705c81b294c4a73b785a0754712fc968eb40
nonce_commit    (point):  032326abcd4eb2fd1a47053ec9ce1aab3ee91e98373d610e9752a7d16a5c1e38d8
D_0             (point):  032326abcd4eb2fd1a47053ec9ce1aab3ee91e98373d610e9752a7d16a5c1e38d8
    (for presentationLimit = 2, k = 1, so only D_0 is published; it equals
     nonce_commit because bases[0] = 1 and D[0] is the only term.)

presentation proof (hex, 321 bytes = Ne + 9*Ns = 33 + 288;
                    layout: nonce_commit_enc(33) || challenge(32) || responses[0..7](8*32)):
    032326abcd4eb2fd1a47053ec9ce1aab3ee91e98373d610e9752a7d16a5c1e38d8   # nonce_commit (Ne bytes)
    946f5f0b44e34f826b41ec59a4e2dcfaa826b8a39cc278e10b1b02b5dbaafdb6     # challenge
    e789639885a8d2d69269a9fea55830f1d7e1fd0a771183b7b4eebe5e03e0c025     # response_0
    5d1ba614de7e31d4f46eb93a24e0ffe9864b002527109a516a10dc1ad718b8d9     # response_1
    84efd16ab245d7a5dfabe2d0027e23796981422b19c2821a831cb46a8e9b8b56     # response_2
    6bbdb55b649021bf2f777b9130c2e375f560eee4691d04bd38e9571d94512578     # response_3
    58d9128002a2f8908d7e4521510a2185244fa533e2502b61e502fd157d974f91     # response_4
    acc4f2ba0d724f2bfd182d5df4d038e74b5c35cc7c4aa7622c2682e040877eeb     # response_5
    cc18fe822cc6abab5d3adc9db836991d3d1ecf699658245b8b0756946ba0d775     # response_6
    6b433aae3b476ccbc2186b2fe2ecc2fe0da30df264802829254df8196a8307f0     # response_7

Presentation 2 (nonce = 1)
--------------------------
a               (scalar): ae14ddaf96907f2fee72069664e1883fee4582cefcfbb2f3fae380c317018ab2
r               (scalar): f994bf66d0c7943ce97331da186e231281b691eb271c7c524ff9f8bc7804b41d
z               (scalar): fa27efc5066bca91121642d629477eb1c7812fa9c473b30dea3eaba8a1731568
nonce_blinding  (scalar): 90b8387fe4145c2d47a0f042c26119939bcbcc8c2c32f81d1034db3958b9af39
nonce           (int):    0x1
U               (point):  0357e53851143e7cc34311bdba0d44d4d3c9192180434ce247b8766232b5de1e08
U_prime_commit  (point):  02bad8dc9b0179dff7a1d63d03d92810520085cbc41b65b667d3cbe2203eb7c544
m1_commit       (point):  02455589d2b92a24e49ff8c2e8287f6eeb05cbfddc16aba66dfe9ab97702bc3c35
tag             (point):  02ad6c293325d0c2c388c8b2240b6d8ab9e52395297ef5921fb78ace6a1274b03b
nonce_commit    (point):  0363d6bd2969b64a42354ba896be33a4abce479261d7dec0001fa1af7fbdeecb41
D_0             (point):  0363d6bd2969b64a42354ba896be33a4abce479261d7dec0001fa1af7fbdeecb41

presentation proof (hex, 321 bytes = Ne + 9*Ns = 33 + 288;
                    layout: nonce_commit_enc(33) || challenge(32) || responses[0..7](8*32)):
    0363d6bd2969b64a42354ba896be33a4abce479261d7dec0001fa1af7fbdeecb41   # nonce_commit (Ne bytes)
    7c59300e0aafb0d58e3f85423030401dabe5dd39566924f07e99cae5b3be62f4     # challenge
    5e736890857cfe0950c22c93c52d56e6ade5f5a1c1d486e9261e7788b7454387     # response_0
    0115370c46e62e376b17844a287bbb6722dc5e5848fdbd8d19d259ec1cec8385     # response_1
    1a1ef4a21dadefeef3f5222eb19361facbb2ec3ba640aef22cd5700a17ea17fc     # response_2
    3ece772f5b5cca1e119bf32cfa7f3459c2184d6d8c777d281c91b416187ee949     # response_3
    c9557fece8afb0ac785c7b8c4854e622f8b005daf5c0682cfdc2d900150087ba     # response_4
    e0090d44b7bac130c7f4067bb8b3374b159106d0c03e30f9577063de0b52d15d     # response_5
    1f5f41328b335ee23d10ca7dc4e0717bd9e919e3f6f580e594b3c48b358baa7a     # response_6
    320ec9e1019260efe2cbf6e9cba6871ffee3b5566d5e865c729c5e1d48529559     # response_7


========================================================================
Field-name mapping (spec <-> WG JSON <-> Apple JSON)
========================================================================
  Draft prose            WG/Apple key         Notes
  ---------              ---------            ---------
  x0, x1, x2             x0, x1, x2           server private keys
  x0Blinding             xb                   blinding scalar for X0
  X0, X1, X2             X0, X1, X2           server public key points
  m1, m2                 m1, m2               client secret scalars
  r1, r2                 r1, r2               El-Gamal blinding scalars
  m1Enc, m2Enc           m1_enc, m2_enc       ElGamal-encrypted m1, m2
  requestProof           CredentialRequest.proof
  b                      b                    response blind scalar
  U                      U
  encUPrime              enc_U_prime
  X0Aux..HAux            X0_aux..H_aux
  responseProof          CredentialResponse.proof
  UPrime                 U_prime              (carried inside Credential after decryption)
  a, r, z                a, r, z              presentation blind scalars
  nonceBlinding          nonce_blinding
  U                      U                    presentation's randomized U
  UPrimeCommit           U_prime_commit
  m1Commit               m1_commit
  tag                    tag
  nonceCommit            nonce_commit
  D[0..k-1]              D_0, D_1, ...        only D_0 here (k = 1)
  presentationProof      Presentation*.proof


========================================================================
Divergences: WG vectors vs Apple swift-crypto
========================================================================
Apple's swift-crypto Tests/CryptoExtrasTests/ARC/ARCTestVectors.swift
(fetched 2026-04-16 from branch main) inlines a DIFFERENT set of vectors.
Every scalar and point in ARCV1-P256 differs — the Apple file appears to
have been generated with a different seed or an earlier ARC spec version.
Examples:

  Field            Draft-01 / WG JSON                                    Apple swift-crypto
  -------          -------------------                                   ----------------
  x0               1008f2c706ae2157c75e41b2d75695c7bf480...              3338fa65ec36e0290022b48eb562889d89dbfa...
  x1               526e009578f6f25fdec992343f09f5e6c584...               f9db001266677f62c095021db018cd8cbb55...
  X1               02a0323862a05707d76862bfa8477eed4684...               03c413230a9bd956718aa46138a33f774f4c...
  request_context  74657374207265717565737420636f6e74657874 (match)
  m2               911fb315...486be6a3e (match!)                         911fb315...486be6a3e (match)

m2 and request_context happen to match, but x0, x1, x2, xb, X0..X2,
m1, r1, r2, b, U, U', X0_aux..H_aux, enc_U_prime, all proofs, and both
Presentations' randomness/outputs DIFFER between the two sources.

Conclusion: use the WG JSON (or the text in Section 10.2 of
draft-01) as the authoritative source. Apple's file is useful only as a
sanity-check of the CODE PATH (its struct shape is identical), but NOT as
cross-implementation bit-level confirmation of draft-01. Apple also
includes ARCV1-P384 vectors, which the draft-01 WG JSON does not (the
draft only publishes ARCV1-P256).

Apple additionally OMITS these fields that the WG JSON publishes:
  - Presentation1/2.D_0
  - Presentation1/2.nonce_blinding
  - Presentation1/2.nonce_commit
(All three are recomputable from the proof bytes and seed, but the WG
JSON spells them out explicitly.)


========================================================================
Implementation notes / wire-format reminders (from draft-01 Section 6)
========================================================================
  Ne (point, compressed)       = 33 bytes for P-256
  Ns (scalar)                   = 32 bytes for P-256
  CredentialRequest on wire     = m1_enc || m2_enc || request_proof = 226 bytes
                                  = 33 + 33 + 160
  CredentialResponse on wire    = U || enc_U_prime || X0_aux || X1_aux || X2_aux || H_aux || response_proof
                                  = 6*33 + 256 = 454 bytes
  Presentation on wire          = U || U_prime_commit || m1_commit || tag || presentation_proof
                                  = 4*33 + 321 = 453 bytes
  presentation_proof layout     = nonce_commit_enc(Ne) || challenge(Ns) || responses(8*Ns)
                                  = 33 + 32 + 256 = 321 bytes (for presentationLimit = 2, k = 1)
  Range-proof D array           = D[0..k-1] with k = ceil(log2(presentationLimit))
                                  For presentationLimit=2, k=1, so only D_0 exists.
                                  For larger limits, additional D_i points appear
                                  inside the proof (scalar count grows accordingly).
